revision.comment: Convert [[Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster]] to wikilink (The bot operation is completed 36.5% in total)
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name =Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | native_name =川南古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption =Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|10|24|N|131|29|48|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kawaminami, Miyazaki]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th to 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|10|24|N|131|29|48|E}}}}}} {{nihongo|'''Kawaminami Kofun Cluster'''|川南古墳群|}} is a group [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]]s, located in the Nishibeppu neighborhood of the town of [[Kawaminami, Miyazaki]] on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1961.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139794|title=川南古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== A group of ancient tombs located on the edge of the Kokkobaru plateau at an elevation of 50 to 60 meters on the left bank of the Komaru River, and on a plateau one step above the elevation of 80 meters. The cluster consists of 25 {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}}''|前方後方墳}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. one {{nihongo|''[[Square Kofun|hōfun]]''|方墳}} rectangular tomb, and 25 {{nihongo|''[[Round Kofun|enpun]]''|円墳}} round tombs within a narrow area of approximately 0.5 square kilometers. There are also several small circular tumuli and undesignated ''kofun'' that are known to have been destroyed, and the area designated as a historical site is limited to the portion containing tumuli that remain visible on the ground. As only a few surveys and [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted, there is a considerable amount of surrounding undesignated area which probably contains more tumuli which remain undiscovered. Since the ratio of [[zenpō-kōen-fun|keyhole-shaped tombs]] in this group is extremely high, it is thought that the tumuli are not the graves of a single chieftain's family, but rather the grave areas of several influential clans over a fairly wide area. The tombs are broadly divided into five groups depending on their location. It is estimated that these tumuli were built between the 4th century and the end of the 6th century, and thus predate the tumuli at the Saitobaru Kofun Cluster. The largest tumulus in the group is Mound No. 39, which has a total length of 112 meters and is surrounded by a moat. It is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Komaru River basin. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From this site the [[Mochida Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 3.5 kilometers to the southeast, the [[Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 9 kilometers to the southwest, and the [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] approximately 8 kilometers to the west-southwest. The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Takanabe Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Kawaminami Kofun Cluster}} *[https://www.town.kawaminami.miyazaki.jp/soshiki/12/4356.html Kawaminami town home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mch/details/view/1850 Miyazaki Cultural Heritage] {{in lang|ja}}

[[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::History of Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Kawaminami, Miyazaki]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kk]] [[en:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | native_name =川南古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption =Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|10|24|N|131|29|48|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kawaminami, Miyazaki]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th to 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|10|24|N|131|29|48|E}}}}}} {{nihongo|'''Kawaminami Kofun Cluster'''|川南古墳群|}} is a group [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]]s, located in the Nishibeppu neighborhood of the town of [[Kawaminami, Miyazaki]] on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1961.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139794|title=川南古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== A group of ancient tombs located on the edge of the Kokkobaru plateau at an elevation of 50 to 60 meters on the left bank of the Komaru River, and on a plateau one step above the elevation of 80 meters. The cluster consists of 25 {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}}''|前方後方墳}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. one {{nihongo|''[[Square Kofun|hōfun]]''|方墳}} rectangular tomb, and 25 {{nihongo|''[[Round Kofun|enpun]]''|円墳}} round tombs within a narrow area of approximately 0.5 square kilometers. There are also several small circular tumuli and undesignated ''kofun'' that are known to have been destroyed, and the area designated as a historical site is limited to the portion containing tumuli that remain visible on the ground. As only a few surveys and [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted, there is a considerable amount of surrounding undesignated area which probably contains more tumuli which remain undiscovered. Since the ratio of [[zenpō-kōen-fun|keyhole-shaped tombs]] in this group is extremely high, it is thought that the tumuli are not the graves of a single chieftain's family, but rather the grave areas of several influential clans over a fairly wide area. The tombs are broadly divided into five groups depending on their location. It is estimated that these tumuli were built between the 4th century and the end of the 6th century, and thus predate the tumuli at the Saitobaru Kofun Cluster. The largest tumulus in the group is Mound No. 39, which has a total length of 112 meters and is surrounded by a moat. It is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Komaru River basin. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From this site the [[Mochida Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 3.5 kilometers to the southeast, the [[Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 9 kilometers to the southwest, and the [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] approximately 8 kilometers to the west-southwest. The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Takanabe Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | native_name =川南古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption =Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|10|24|N|131|29|48|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kawaminami, Miyazaki]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th to 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|10|24|N|131|29|48|E}}}}}} {{nihongo|'''Kawaminami Kofun Cluster'''|川南古墳群|}} is a group [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]]s, located in the Nishibeppu neighborhood of the town of [[Kawaminami, Miyazaki]] on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1961.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139794|title=川南古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== A group of ancient tombs located on the edge of the Kokkobaru plateau at an elevation of 50 to 60 meters on the left bank of the Komaru River, and on a plateau one step above the elevation of 80 meters. The cluster consists of 25 {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}}''|前方後方墳}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. one {{nihongo|''[[Square Kofun|hōfun]]''|方墳}} rectangular tomb, and 25 {{nihongo|''[[Round Kofun|enpun]]''|円墳}} round tombs within a narrow area of approximately 0.5 square kilometers. There are also several small circular tumuli and undesignated ''kofun'' that are known to have been destroyed, and the area designated as a historical site is limited to the portion containing tumuli that remain visible on the ground. As only a few surveys and [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted, there is a considerable amount of surrounding undesignated area which probably contains more tumuli which remain undiscovered. Since the ratio of [[zenpō-kōen-fun|keyhole-shaped tombs]] in this group is extremely high, it is thought that the tumuli are not the graves of a single chieftain's family, but rather the grave areas of several influential clans over a fairly wide area. The tombs are broadly divided into five groups depending on their location. It is estimated that these tumuli were built between the 4th century and the end of the 6th century, and thus predate the tumuli at the Saitobaru Kofun Cluster. The largest tumulus in the group is Mound No. 39, which has a total length of 112 meters and is surrounded by a moat. It is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Komaru River basin. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From this site the [[Mochida Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 3.5 kilometers to the southeast, the [[Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 9 kilometers to the southwest, and the [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] approximately 8 kilometers to the west-southwest. The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Takanabe Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)]]

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後方墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後方墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name =Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | native_name =川南古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption =Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|10|24|N|131|29|48|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kawaminami, Miyazaki]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th to 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|10|24|N|131|29|48|E}}}}}} {{nihongo|'''Kawaminami Kofun Cluster'''|川南古墳群|}} is a group [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]]s, located in the Nishibeppu neighborhood of the town of [[Kawaminami, Miyazaki]] on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1961.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139794|title=川南古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== A group of ancient tombs located on the edge of the Kokkobaru plateau at an elevation of 50 to 60 meters on the left bank of the Komaru River, and on a plateau one step above the elevation of 80 meters. The cluster consists of 25 {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}}''|前方後方墳}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. one {{nihongo|''[[Square Kofun|hōfun]]''|方墳}} rectangular tomb, and 25 {{nihongo|''[[Round Kofun|enpun]]''|円墳}} round tombs within a narrow area of approximately 0.5 square kilometers. There are also several small circular tumuli and undesignated ''kofun'' that are known to have been destroyed, and the area designated as a historical site is limited to the portion containing tumuli that remain visible on the ground. As only a few surveys and [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted, there is a considerable amount of surrounding undesignated area which probably contains more tumuli which remain undiscovered. Since the ratio of [[zenpō-kōen-fun|keyhole-shaped tombs]] in this group is extremely high, it is thought that the tumuli are not the graves of a single chieftain's family, but rather the grave areas of several influential clans over a fairly wide area. The tombs are broadly divided into five groups depending on their location. It is estimated that these tumuli were built between the 4th century and the end of the 6th century, and thus predate the tumuli at the Saitobaru Kofun Cluster. The largest tumulus in the group is Mound No. 39, which has a total length of 112 meters and is surrounded by a moat. It is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Komaru River basin. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From this site the [[Mochida Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 3.5 kilometers to the southeast, the [[Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 9 kilometers to the southwest, and the [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] approximately 8 kilometers to the west-southwest. The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Takanabe Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Kawaminami Kofun Cluster}} *[https://www.town.kawaminami.miyazaki.jp/soshiki/12/4356.html Kawaminami town home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mch/details/view/1850 Miyazaki Cultural Heritage] {{in lang|ja}}

The cluster consists of 25 {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. one {{nihongo|''[[Square Kofun|hōfun]]''|方墳}} rectangular tomb, and 25 {{nihongo|''[[Round Kofun|enpun]]''|円墳}} round tombs within a narrow area of approximately 0.5 square kilometers. There are also several small circular tumuli and undesignated ''kofun'' that are known to have been destroyed, and the area designated as a historical site is limited to the portion containing tumuli that remain visible on the ground. As only a few surveys and [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted, there is a considerable amount of surrounding undesignated area which probably contains more tumuli which remain undiscovered. Since the ratio of [[zenpō-kōen-fun|keyhole-shaped tombs]] in this group is extremely high, it is thought that the tumuli are not the graves of a single chieftain's family, but rather the grave areas of several influential clans over a fairly wide area. The tombs are broadly divided into five groups depending on their location. It is estimated that these tumuli were built between the 4th century and the end of the 6th century, and thus predate the tumuli at the Saitobaru Kofun Cluster. The largest tumulus in the group is Mound No. 39, which has a total length of 112 meters and is surrounded by a moat. It is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Komaru River basin. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kawaminami, Miyazaki]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]]

[[Category:kk]] [[en:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]][[Category:円墳]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]][[Category:円墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:前方後方墳]][[sn:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]][[Category:前方後方墳]] [[Category:前方後方墳]][[sn:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]][[Category:前方後方墳]] [[Category:前方後方墳]][[sn:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]][[Category:前方後方墳]] [[Category:前方後方墳]][[sn:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]][[Category:前方後方墳]] [[Category:completion tier 4]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | native_name =川南古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption =Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|10|24|N|131|29|48|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kawaminami, Miyazaki]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th to 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|10|24|N|131|29|48|E}}}}}} {{nihongo|'''Kawaminami Kofun Cluster'''|川南古墳群|}} is a group [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]]s, located in the Nishibeppu neighborhood of the town of [[Kawaminami, Miyazaki]] on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1961.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139794|title=川南古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== A group of ancient tombs located on the edge of the Kokkobaru plateau at an elevation of 50 to 60 meters on the left bank of the Komaru River, and on a plateau one step above the elevation of 80 meters. The cluster consists of 25 {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. one {{nihongo|''[[Square Kofun|hōfun]]''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} rectangular tomb, and 25 {{nihongo|''[[Round Kofun|enpun]]''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} round tombs within a narrow area of approximately 0.5 square kilometers. There are also several small circular tumuli and undesignated ''kofun'' that are known to have been destroyed, and the area designated as a historical site is limited to the portion containing tumuli that remain visible on the ground. As only a few surveys and [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted, there is a considerable amount of surrounding undesignated area which probably contains more tumuli which remain undiscovered. Since the ratio of [[zenpō-kōen-fun|keyhole-shaped tombs]] in this group is extremely high, it is thought that the tumuli are not the graves of a single chieftain's family, but rather the grave areas of several influential clans over a fairly wide area. The tombs are broadly divided into five groups depending on their location. It is estimated that these tumuli were built between the 4th century and the end of the 6th century, and thus predate the tumuli at the Saitobaru Kofun Cluster. The largest tumulus in the group is Mound No. 39, which has a total length of 112 meters and is surrounded by a moat. It is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Komaru River basin. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From this site the [[Mochida Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 3.5 kilometers to the southeast, the [[Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 9 kilometers to the southwest, and the [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] approximately 8 kilometers to the west-southwest. The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Takanabe Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | native_name =川南古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption =Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|10|24|N|131|29|48|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kawaminami, Miyazaki]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th to 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|10|24|N|131|29|48|E}}}}}} {{nihongo|'''Kawaminami Kofun Cluster'''|川南古墳群|}} is a group [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Nishibeppu neighborhood of the town of [[Kawaminami, Miyazaki]] on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1961.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139794|title=川南古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== A group of ancient tombs located on the edge of the Kokkobaru plateau at an elevation of 50 to 60 meters on the left bank of the Komaru River, and on a plateau one step above the elevation of 80 meters. The cluster consists of 25 {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. one {{nihongo|''[[Square Kofun|hōfun]]''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} rectangular tomb, and 25 {{nihongo|''[[Round Kofun|enpun]]''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} round tombs within a narrow area of approximately 0.5 square kilometers. There are also several small circular tumuli and undesignated ''kofun'' that are known to have been destroyed, and the area designated as a historical site is limited to the portion containing tumuli that remain visible on the ground. As only a few surveys and [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted, there is a considerable amount of surrounding undesignated area which probably contains more tumuli which remain undiscovered. Since the ratio of [[zenpō-kōen-fun|keyhole-shaped tombs]] in this group is extremely high, it is thought that the tumuli are not the graves of a single chieftain's family, but rather the grave areas of several influential clans over a fairly wide area. The tombs are broadly divided into five groups depending on their location. It is estimated that these tumuli were built between the 4th century and the end of the 6th century, and thus predate the tumuli at the Saitobaru Kofun Cluster. The largest tumulus in the group is Mound No. 39, which has a total length of 112 meters and is surrounded by a moat. It is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Komaru River basin. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From this site the [[Mochida Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 3.5 kilometers to the southeast, the [[Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 9 kilometers to the southwest, and the [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] approximately 8 kilometers to the west-southwest. The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Takanabe Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:11]][[Category:-inline|Kawaminami Kofun Cluster}} *[https://www.town.kawaminami.miyazaki.jp/soshiki/12/4356.html Kawaminami town home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mch/details/view/1850 Miyazaki Cultural Heritage] {{in lang|ja}}

As only a few surveys and [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted, there is a considerable amount of surrounding undesignated area which probably contains more tumuli which remain undiscovered. Since the ratio of [[zenpō-kōen-fun|keyhole-shaped tombs]] in this group is extremely high, it is thought that the tumuli are not the graves of a single chieftain's family, but rather the grave areas of several influential clans over a fairly wide area. The tombs are broadly divided into five groups depending on their location. It is estimated that these tumuli were built between the 4th century and the end of the 6th century, and thus predate the tumuli at the Saitobaru Kofun Cluster. The largest tumulus in the group is Mound No. 39, which has a total length of 112 meters and is surrounded by a moat. It is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Komaru River basin. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From this site the [[Mochida Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 3.5 kilometers to the southeast, the [[Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 9 kilometers to the southwest, and the [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] approximately 8 kilometers to the west-southwest. The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Takanabe Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | native_name =川南古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption =Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|10|24|N|131|29|48|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kawaminami, Miyazaki]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th to 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|10|24|N|131|29|48|E}}}}}} {{nihongo|'''Kawaminami Kofun Cluster'''|川南古墳群|}} is a group [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Nishibeppu neighborhood of the town of [[Kawaminami, Miyazaki]] on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1961.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139794|title=川南古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== A group of ancient tombs located on the edge of the Kokkobaru plateau at an elevation of 50 to 60 meters on the left bank of the Komaru River, and on a plateau one step above the elevation of 80 meters. The cluster consists of 25 {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. one {{nihongo|''[[Square Kofun|hōfun]]''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} rectangular tomb, and 25 {{nihongo|''[[Round Kofun|enpun]]''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} round tombs within a narrow area of approximately 0.5 square kilometers. There are also several small circular tumuli and undesignated ''kofun'' that are known to have been destroyed, and the area designated as a historical site is limited to the portion containing tumuli that remain visible on the ground. As only a few surveys and [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted, there is a considerable amount of surrounding undesignated area which probably contains more tumuli which remain undiscovered. Since the ratio of [[zenpō-kōen-fun|keyhole-shaped tombs]] in this group is extremely high, it is thought that the tumuli are not the graves of a single chieftain's family, but rather the grave areas of several influential clans over a fairly wide area. The tombs are broadly divided into five groups depending on their location. It is estimated that these tumuli were built between the 4th century and the end of the 6th century, and thus predate the tumuli at the Saitobaru Kofun Cluster. The largest tumulus in the group is Mound No. 39, which has a total length of 112 meters and is surrounded by a moat. It is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Komaru River basin. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From this site the [[Mochida Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 3.5 kilometers to the southeast, the [[Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 9 kilometers to the southwest, and the [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] approximately 8 kilometers to the west-southwest. The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Takanabe Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | native_name =川南古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption =Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|10|24|N|131|29|48|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kawaminami, Miyazaki]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th to 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|10|24|N|131|29|48|E}}}}}} {{nihongo|'''Kawaminami Kofun Cluster'''|川南古墳群|}} is a group [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Nishibeppu neighborhood of the town of [[Kawaminami, Miyazaki]] on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1961.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139794|title=川南古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== A group of ancient tombs located on the edge of the Kokkobaru plateau at an elevation of 50 to 60 meters on the left bank of the Komaru River, and on a plateau one step above the elevation of 80 meters. The cluster consists of 25 {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. one {{nihongo|''[[Square Kofun|hōfun]]''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} rectangular tomb, and 25 {{nihongo|''[[Round Kofun|enpun]]''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} round tombs within a narrow area of approximately 0.5 square kilometers. There are also several small circular tumuli and undesignated ''kofun'' that are known to have been destroyed, and the area designated as a historical site is limited to the portion containing tumuli that remain visible on the ground. As only a few surveys and [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted, there is a considerable amount of surrounding undesignated area which probably contains more tumuli which remain undiscovered. Since the ratio of [[zenpō-kōen-fun|keyhole-shaped tombs]] in this group is extremely high, it is thought that the tumuli are not the graves of a single chieftain's family, but rather the grave areas of several influential clans over a fairly wide area. The tombs are broadly divided into five groups depending on their location. It is estimated that these tumuli were built between the 4th century and the end of the 6th century, and thus predate the tumuli at the Saitobaru Kofun Cluster. The largest tumulus in the group is Mound No. 39, which has a total length of 112 meters and is surrounded by a moat. It is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Komaru River basin. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From this site the [[Mochida Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 3.5 kilometers to the southeast, the [[Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 9 kilometers to the southwest, and the [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] approximately 8 kilometers to the west-southwest. The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Takanabe Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:11]][[Category:-inline|Kawaminami Kofun Cluster}} *[https://www.town.kawaminami.miyazaki.jp/soshiki/12/4356.html Kawaminami town home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mch/details/view/1850 Miyazaki Cultural Heritage] {{in lang|ja}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:11]] [[Category:History of Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Kawaminami, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] }} [[en:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]] [[sn:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]]

{{draft categories| [[Category:11]] [[Category:History of Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Kawaminami, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] }} [[en:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]] [[sn:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | native_name =川南古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption =Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|10|24|N|131|29|48|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kawaminami, Miyazaki]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th to 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|10|24|N|131|29|48|E}}}}}} {{nihongo|'''Kawaminami Kofun Cluster'''|川南古墳群|}} is a group [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Nishibeppu neighborhood of the town of [[Kawaminami, Miyazaki]] on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1961.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139794|title=川南古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== A group of ancient tombs located on the edge of the Kokkobaru plateau at an elevation of 50 to 60 meters on the left bank of the Komaru River, and on a plateau one step above the elevation of 80 meters. The cluster consists of 25 {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. one {{nihongo|''[[Square Kofun|hōfun]]''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} rectangular tomb, and 25 {{nihongo|''[[Round Kofun|enpun]]''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} round tombs within a narrow area of approximately 0.5 square kilometers. There are also several small circular tumuli and undesignated ''kofun'' that are known to have been destroyed, and the area designated as a historical site is limited to the portion containing tumuli that remain visible on the ground. As only a few surveys and [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted, there is a considerable amount of surrounding undesignated area which probably contains more tumuli which remain undiscovered. Since the ratio of [[zenpō-kōen-fun|keyhole-shaped tombs]] in this group is extremely high, it is thought that the tumuli are not the graves of a single chieftain's family, but rather the grave areas of several influential clans over a fairly wide area. The tombs are broadly divided into five groups depending on their location. It is estimated that these tumuli were built between the 4th century and the end of the 6th century, and thus predate the tumuli at the Saitobaru Kofun Cluster. The largest tumulus in the group is Mound No. 39, which has a total length of 112 meters and is surrounded by a moat. It is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Komaru River basin. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From this site the [[Mochida Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 3.5 kilometers to the southeast, the [[Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 9 kilometers to the southwest, and the [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] approximately 8 kilometers to the west-southwest. The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Takanabe Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

From this site the [[Mochida Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 3.5 kilometers to the southeast, the [[Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 9 kilometers to the southwest, and the [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] approximately 8 kilometers to the west-southwest. The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Takanabe Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:11]][[Category:-inline|Kawaminami Kofun Cluster}} *[https://www.town.kawaminami.miyazaki.jp/soshiki/12/4356.html Kawaminami town home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mch/details/view/1850 Miyazaki Cultural Heritage] {{in lang|ja}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Kawaminami, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] }} [[en:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]] [[sn:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]] {{citation needed|Kawaminami Kofun Cluster}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]]
[[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | native_name =川南古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption =Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|10|24|N|131|29|48|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kawaminami, Miyazaki]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th to 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|10|24|N|131|29|48|E}}}}}} {{nihongo|'''Kawaminami Kofun Cluster'''|川南古墳群|}} is a group [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Nishibeppu neighborhood of the town of [[Kawaminami, Miyazaki]] on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1961.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139794|title=川南古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== A group of ancient tombs located on the edge of the Kokkobaru plateau at an elevation of 50 to 60 meters on the left bank of the Komaru River, and on a plateau one step above the elevation of 80 meters. The cluster consists of 25 {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. one {{nihongo|''[[Square Kofun|hōfun]]''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} rectangular tomb, and 25 {{nihongo|''[[Round Kofun|enpun]]''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} round tombs within a narrow area of approximately 0.5 square kilometers. There are also several small circular tumuli and undesignated ''kofun'' that are known to have been destroyed, and the area designated as a historical site is limited to the portion containing tumuli that remain visible on the ground. As only a few surveys and [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted, there is a considerable amount of surrounding undesignated area which probably contains more tumuli which remain undiscovered. Since the ratio of [[zenpō-kōen-fun|keyhole-shaped tombs]] in this group is extremely high, it is thought that the tumuli are not the graves of a single chieftain's family, but rather the grave areas of several influential clans over a fairly wide area. The tombs are broadly divided into five groups depending on their location. It is estimated that these tumuli were built between the 4th century and the end of the 6th century, and thus predate the tumuli at the Saitobaru Kofun Cluster. The largest tumulus in the group is Mound No. 39, which has a total length of 112 meters and is surrounded by a moat. It is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Komaru River basin. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From this site the [[Mochida Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 3.5 kilometers to the southeast, the [[Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 9 kilometers to the southwest, and the [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] approximately 8 kilometers to the west-southwest. The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Takanabe Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:11]][[Category:-inline|Kawaminami Kofun Cluster}} *[https://www.town.kawaminami.miyazaki.jp/soshiki/12/4356.html Kawaminami town home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mch/details/view/1850 Miyazaki Cultural Heritage] {{in lang|ja}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Kawaminami, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] }} [[en:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]] [[sn:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]] {{citation needed|Kawaminami Kofun Cluster}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]][[en:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]] {{Infobox ancient site | name =Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | native_name =川南古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption =Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|10|24|N|131|29|48|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kawaminami, Miyazaki|Kawaminami, Miyazaki]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th to 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|10|24|N|131|29|48|E}}}}}} {{nihongo|'''Kawaminami Kofun Cluster'''|川南古墳群|}} is a group [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Nishibeppu neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Kawaminami, Miyazaki|Kawaminami, Miyazaki]] on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1961.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139794|title=川南古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== A group of ancient tombs located on the edge of the Kokkobaru plateau at an elevation of 50 to 60 meters on the left bank of the Komaru River, and on a plateau one step above the elevation of 80 meters. The cluster consists of 25 {{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. one {{nihongo|''[[Square Kofun|hōfun]]''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} rectangular tomb, and 25 {{nihongo|''[[Round Kofun|enpun]]''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} round tombs within a narrow area of approximately 0.5 square kilometers. There are also several small circular tumuli and undesignated ''kofun'' that are known to have been destroyed, and the area designated as a historical site is limited to the portion containing tumuli that remain visible on the ground. As only a few surveys and [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted, there is a considerable amount of surrounding undesignated area which probably contains more tumuli which remain undiscovered. Since the ratio of [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tombs]] in this group is extremely high, it is thought that the tumuli are not the graves of a single chieftain's family, but rather the grave areas of several influential clans over a fairly wide area. The tombs are broadly divided into five groups depending on their location. It is estimated that these tumuli were built between the 4th century and the end of the 6th century, and thus predate the tumuli at the Saitobaru Kofun Cluster. The largest tumulus in the group is Mound No. 39, which has a total length of 112 meters and is surrounded by a moat. It is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Komaru River basin. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From this site the [[Mochida Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 3.5 kilometers to the southeast, the [[Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 9 kilometers to the southwest, and the [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] approximately 8 kilometers to the west-southwest. The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[:en:Takanabe Station|Takanabe Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]][[en:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]] {{Infobox ancient site | name =Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | native_name =川南古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption =Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|10|24|N|131|29|48|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kawaminami, Miyazaki|Kawaminami, Miyazaki]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th to 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|10|24|N|131|29|48|E}}}}}} {{nihongo|'''Kawaminami Kofun Cluster'''|川南古墳群|}} is a group [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Nishibeppu neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Kawaminami, Miyazaki|Kawaminami, Miyazaki]] on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1961.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139794|title=川南古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== A group of ancient tombs located on the edge of the Kokkobaru plateau at an elevation of 50 to 60 meters on the left bank of the Komaru River, and on a plateau one step above the elevation of 80 meters. The cluster consists of 25 {{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. one {{nihongo|''[[Square Kofun|hōfun]]''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} rectangular tomb, and 25 {{nihongo|''[[Round Kofun|enpun]]''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} round tombs within a narrow area of approximately 0.5 square kilometers. There are also several small circular tumuli and undesignated ''kofun'' that are known to have been destroyed, and the area designated as a historical site is limited to the portion containing tumuli that remain visible on the ground. As only a few surveys and [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted, there is a considerable amount of surrounding undesignated area which probably contains more tumuli which remain undiscovered. Since the ratio of [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tombs]] in this group is extremely high, it is thought that the tumuli are not the graves of a single chieftain's family, but rather the grave areas of several influential clans over a fairly wide area. The tombs are broadly divided into five groups depending on their location. It is estimated that these tumuli were built between the 4th century and the end of the 6th century, and thus predate the tumuli at the Saitobaru Kofun Cluster. The largest tumulus in the group is Mound No. 39, which has a total length of 112 meters and is surrounded by a moat. It is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Komaru River basin. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From this site the [[Mochida Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 3.5 kilometers to the southeast, the [[Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 9 kilometers to the southwest, and the [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] approximately 8 kilometers to the west-southwest. The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[:en:Takanabe Station|Takanabe Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:11]][[Category:-inline|Kawaminami Kofun Cluster}} *[https://www.town.kawaminami.miyazaki.jp/soshiki/12/4356.html Kawaminami town home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mch/details/view/1850 Miyazaki Cultural Heritage] {{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]][[en:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]] {{Infobox ancient site | name =Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | native_name =川南古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption =Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|10|24|N|131|29|48|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kawaminami, Miyazaki|Kawaminami, Miyazaki]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th to 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|10|24|N|131|29|48|E}}}}}} {{nihongo|'''Kawaminami Kofun Cluster'''|川南古墳群|}} is a group [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Nishibeppu neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Kawaminami, Miyazaki|Kawaminami, Miyazaki]] on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1961.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139794|title=川南古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== A group of ancient tombs located on the edge of the Kokkobaru plateau at an elevation of 50 to 60 meters on the left bank of the Komaru River, and on a plateau one step above the elevation of 80 meters. The cluster consists of 25 {{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. one {{nihongo|''[[Square Kofun|hōfun]]''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} rectangular tomb, and 25 {{nihongo|''[[Round Kofun|enpun]]''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} round tombs within a narrow area of approximately 0.5 square kilometers. There are also several small circular tumuli and undesignated ''kofun'' that are known to have been destroyed, and the area designated as a historical site is limited to the portion containing tumuli that remain visible on the ground. As only a few surveys and [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted, there is a considerable amount of surrounding undesignated area which probably contains more tumuli which remain undiscovered. Since the ratio of [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tombs]] in this group is extremely high, it is thought that the tumuli are not the graves of a single chieftain's family, but rather the grave areas of several influential clans over a fairly wide area. The tombs are broadly divided into five groups depending on their location. It is estimated that these tumuli were built between the 4th century and the end of the 6th century, and thus predate the tumuli at the Saitobaru Kofun Cluster. The largest tumulus in the group is Mound No. 39, which has a total length of 112 meters and is surrounded by a moat. It is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Komaru River basin. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From this site the [[Mochida Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 3.5 kilometers to the southeast, the [[Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 9 kilometers to the southwest, and the [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] approximately 8 kilometers to the west-southwest. The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[:en:Takanabe Station|Takanabe Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:11]][[Category:-inline|Kawaminami Kofun Cluster}} *[https://www.town.kawaminami.miyazaki.jp/soshiki/12/4356.html Kawaminami town home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mch/details/view/1850 Miyazaki Cultural Heritage] {{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]][[en:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]][[en:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]][[en:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]] {{Infobox ancient site | name =Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | native_name =川南古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption =Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|10|24|N|131|29|48|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kawaminami, Miyazaki|Kawaminami, Miyazaki]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th to 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|10|24|N|131|29|48|E}}}}}} {{nihongo|'''Kawaminami Kofun Cluster'''|川南古墳群|}} is a group [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Nishibeppu neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Kawaminami, Miyazaki|Kawaminami, Miyazaki]] on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1961.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139794|title=川南古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== A group of ancient tombs located on the edge of the Kokkobaru plateau at an elevation of 50 to 60 meters on the left bank of the Komaru River, and on a plateau one step above the elevation of 80 meters. The cluster consists of 25 {{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. one {{nihongo|''[[Square Kofun|hōfun]]''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} rectangular tomb, and 25 {{nihongo|''[[Round Kofun|enpun]]''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} round tombs within a narrow area of approximately 0.5 square kilometers. There are also several small circular tumuli and undesignated ''kofun'' that are known to have been destroyed, and the area designated as a historical site is limited to the portion containing tumuli that remain visible on the ground. As only a few surveys and [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted, there is a considerable amount of surrounding undesignated area which probably contains more tumuli which remain undiscovered. Since the ratio of [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tombs]] in this group is extremely high, it is thought that the tumuli are not the graves of a single chieftain's family, but rather the grave areas of several influential clans over a fairly wide area. The tombs are broadly divided into five groups depending on their location. It is estimated that these tumuli were built between the 4th century and the end of the 6th century, and thus predate the tumuli at the Saitobaru Kofun Cluster. The largest tumulus in the group is Mound No. 39, which has a total length of 112 meters and is surrounded by a moat. It is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Komaru River basin. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From this site the [[Mochida Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 3.5 kilometers to the southeast, the [[Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 9 kilometers to the southwest, and the [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] approximately 8 kilometers to the west-southwest. The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[:en:Takanabe Station|Takanabe Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)]]

The cluster consists of 25 {{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. one {{nihongo|''[[Square Kofun|hōfun]]''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} rectangular tomb, and 25 {{nihongo|''[[Round Kofun|enpun]]''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} round tombs within a narrow area of approximately 0.5 square kilometers. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]][[en:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]][[en:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]][[en:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]] {{Infobox ancient site | name =Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | native_name =川南古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption =Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|10|24|N|131|29|48|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kawaminami, Miyazaki|Kawaminami, Miyazaki]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th to 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|10|24|N|131|29|48|E}}}}}} {{nihongo|'''Kawaminami Kofun Cluster'''|川南古墳群|}} is a group [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Nishibeppu neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Kawaminami, Miyazaki|Kawaminami, Miyazaki]] on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1961.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139794|title=川南古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== A group of ancient tombs located on the edge of the Kokkobaru plateau at an elevation of 50 to 60 meters on the left bank of the Komaru River, and on a plateau one step above the elevation of 80 meters. The cluster consists of 25 {{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. one {{nihongo|''[[Square Kofun|hōfun]]''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} rectangular tomb, and 25 {{nihongo|''[[Round Kofun|enpun]]''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} round tombs within a narrow area of approximately 0.5 square kilometers. There are also several small circular tumuli and undesignated ''kofun'' that are known to have been destroyed, and the area designated as a historical site is limited to the portion containing tumuli that remain visible on the ground. As only a few surveys and [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted, there is a considerable amount of surrounding undesignated area which probably contains more tumuli which remain undiscovered. Since the ratio of [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tombs]] in this group is extremely high, it is thought that the tumuli are not the graves of a single chieftain's family, but rather the grave areas of several influential clans over a fairly wide area. The tombs are broadly divided into five groups depending on their location. It is estimated that these tumuli were built between the 4th century and the end of the 6th century, and thus predate the tumuli at the Saitobaru Kofun Cluster. The largest tumulus in the group is Mound No. 39, which has a total length of 112 meters and is surrounded by a moat. It is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Komaru River basin. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From this site the [[Mochida Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 3.5 kilometers to the southeast, the [[Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 9 kilometers to the southwest, and the [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] approximately 8 kilometers to the west-southwest. The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[:en:Takanabe Station|Takanabe Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)]]
