revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]][[en:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]][[en:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]][[en:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]] {{Infobox ancient site | name =Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | native_name =川南古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption =Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|10|24|N|131|29|48|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kawaminami, Miyazaki|en|Kawaminami, Miyazaki|WD=Q964194|lt=Kawaminami, Miyazaki}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th to 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|10|24|N|131|29|48|E}}}}}} {{nihongo|'''Kawaminami Kofun Cluster'''|川南古墳群|}} is a group [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Nishibeppu neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Kawaminami, Miyazaki|en|Kawaminami, Miyazaki|WD=Q964194|lt=Kawaminami, Miyazaki}} on the island of [[Kyushu]] {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus cluster was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1961.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139794|title=川南古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== A group of ancient tombs located on the edge of the Kokkobaru plateau at an elevation of 50 to 60 meters on the left bank of the Komaru River, and on a plateau one step above the elevation of 80 meters. The cluster consists of 25 {{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. one {{nihongo|''[[Square Kofun|hōfun]]''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} rectangular tomb, and 25 {{nihongo|''[[Round Kofun|enpun]]''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} round tombs within a narrow area of approximately 0.5 square kilometers. There are also several small circular tumuli and undesignated ''kofun'' that are known to have been destroyed, and the area designated as a historical site is limited to the portion containing tumuli that remain visible on the ground. As only a few surveys and [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted, there is a considerable amount of surrounding undesignated area which probably contains more tumuli which remain undiscovered. Since the ratio of {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped tombs}} in this group is extremely high, it is thought that the tumuli are not the graves of a single chieftain's family, but rather the grave areas of several influential clans over a fairly wide area. The tombs are broadly divided into five groups depending on their location. It is estimated that these tumuli were built between the 4th century and the end of the 6th century, and thus predate the tumuli at the Saitobaru Kofun Cluster. The largest tumulus in the group is Mound No. 39, which has a total length of 112 meters and is surrounded by a moat. It is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Komaru River basin. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From this site the [[Mochida Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 3.5 kilometers to the southeast, the [[Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 9 kilometers to the southwest, and the [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] approximately 8 kilometers to the west-southwest. The site is approximately ten minutes by car from {{ill|Takanabe Station|en|Takanabe Station|WD=Q5357588|lt=Takanabe Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|WD=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|WD=Q6573277|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:11]][[Category:-inline|Kawaminami Kofun Cluster}} *[https://www.town.kawaminami.miyazaki.jp/soshiki/12/4356.html Kawaminami town home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mch/details/view/1850 Miyazaki Cultural Heritage] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}
[[en:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]] <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Kawaminami Kofun Cluster}} {{wikidata link|Q123221104}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Kawaminami, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages November 4 2025]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

The site is approximately ten minutes by car from {{ill|Takanabe Station|en|Takanabe Station|WD=Q5357588|lt=Takanabe Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|WD=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]][[en:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]][[en:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]][[en:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]] {{Infobox ancient site | name =Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | native_name =川南古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption =Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|10|24|N|131|29|48|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kawaminami, Miyazaki|en|Kawaminami, Miyazaki|WD=Q964194|lt=Kawaminami, Miyazaki}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th to 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|10|24|N|131|29|48|E}}}}}} {{nihongo|'''Kawaminami Kofun Cluster'''|川南古墳群|}} is a group [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Nishibeppu neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Kawaminami, Miyazaki|en|Kawaminami, Miyazaki|WD=Q964194|lt=Kawaminami, Miyazaki}} on the island of [[Kyushu]] {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus cluster was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1961.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139794|title=川南古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== A group of ancient tombs located on the edge of the Kokkobaru plateau at an elevation of 50 to 60 meters on the left bank of the Komaru River, and on a plateau one step above the elevation of 80 meters. The cluster consists of 25 {{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. one {{nihongo|''[[Square Kofun|hōfun]]''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} rectangular tomb, and 25 {{nihongo|''[[Round Kofun|enpun]]''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} round tombs within a narrow area of approximately 0.5 square kilometers. There are also several small circular tumuli and undesignated ''kofun'' that are known to have been destroyed, and the area designated as a historical site is limited to the portion containing tumuli that remain visible on the ground. As only a few surveys and [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted, there is a considerable amount of surrounding undesignated area which probably contains more tumuli which remain undiscovered. Since the ratio of {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped tombs}} in this group is extremely high, it is thought that the tumuli are not the graves of a single chieftain's family, but rather the grave areas of several influential clans over a fairly wide area. The tombs are broadly divided into five groups depending on their location. It is estimated that these tumuli were built between the 4th century and the end of the 6th century, and thus predate the tumuli at the Saitobaru Kofun Cluster. The largest tumulus in the group is Mound No. 39, which has a total length of 112 meters and is surrounded by a moat. It is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Komaru River basin. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From this site the [[Mochida Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 3.5 kilometers to the southeast, the [[Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 9 kilometers to the southwest, and the [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] approximately 8 kilometers to the west-southwest. The site is approximately ten minutes by car from {{ill|Takanabe Station|en|Takanabe Station|WD=Q5357588|lt=Takanabe Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|WD=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

There are also several small circular tumuli and undesignated ''kofun'' that are known to have been destroyed, and the area designated as a historical site is limited to the portion containing tumuli that remain visible on the ground. As only a few surveys and [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted, there is a considerable amount of surrounding undesignated area which probably contains more tumuli which remain undiscovered. Since the ratio of {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped tombs}} in this group is extremely high, it is thought that the tumuli are not the graves of a single chieftain's family, but rather the grave areas of several influential clans over a fairly wide area. The tombs are broadly divided into five groups depending on their location. It is estimated that these tumuli were built between the 4th century and the end of the 6th century, and thus predate the tumuli at the Saitobaru Kofun Cluster. The largest tumulus in the group is Mound No. 39, which has a total length of 112 meters and is surrounded by a moat. It is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Komaru River basin. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From this site the [[Mochida Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 3.5 kilometers to the southeast, the [[Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 9 kilometers to the southwest, and the [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] approximately 8 kilometers to the west-southwest. The site is approximately ten minutes by car from {{ill|Takanabe Station|en|Takanabe Station|WD=Q5357588|lt=Takanabe Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|WD=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|WD=Q6573277|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)}}
==References== {{reflist}}

{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|10|24|N|131|29|48|E}}}}}} {{nihongo|'''Kawaminami Kofun Cluster'''|川南古墳群|}} is a group [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Nishibeppu neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Kawaminami, Miyazaki|en|Kawaminami, Miyazaki|WD=Q964194|lt=Kawaminami, Miyazaki|1=Kawaminami, Miyazaki}} on the island of [[Kyushu]] {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus cluster was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1961.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139794|title=川南古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== A group of ancient tombs located on the edge of the Kokkobaru plateau at an elevation of 50 to 60 meters on the left bank of the Komaru River, and on a plateau one step above the elevation of 80 meters. The cluster consists of 25 {{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. one {{nihongo|''[[Square Kofun|hōfun]]''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} rectangular tomb, and 25 {{nihongo|''[[Round Kofun|enpun]]''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} round tombs within a narrow area of approximately 0.5 square kilometers. There are also several small circular tumuli and undesignated ''kofun'' that are known to have been destroyed, and the area designated as a historical site is limited to the portion containing tumuli that remain visible on the ground. As only a few surveys and [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted, there is a considerable amount of surrounding undesignated area which probably contains more tumuli which remain undiscovered. Since the ratio of {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped tombs|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} in this group is extremely high, it is thought that the tumuli are not the graves of a single chieftain's family, but rather the grave areas of several influential clans over a fairly wide area. The tombs are broadly divided into five groups depending on their location. It is estimated that these tumuli were built between the 4th century and the end of the 6th century, and thus predate the tumuli at the Saitobaru Kofun Cluster. The largest tumulus in the group is Mound No. 39, which has a total length of 112 meters and is surrounded by a moat. It is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Komaru River basin. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From this site the [[Mochida Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 3.5 kilometers to the southeast, the [[Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 9 kilometers to the southwest, and the [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] approximately 8 kilometers to the west-southwest. The site is approximately ten minutes by car from {{ill|Takanabe Station|en|Takanabe Station|WD=Q5357588|lt=Takanabe Station|1=Takanabe Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|WD=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line|1=Nippō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The site is approximately ten minutes by car from {{ill|Takanabe Station|en|Takanabe Station|WD=Q5357588|lt=Takanabe Station|1=Takanabe Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|WD=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line|1=Nippō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]][[en:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]][[en:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]][[en:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]] {{Infobox ancient site | name =Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | native_name =川南古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption =Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|10|24|N|131|29|48|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kawaminami, Miyazaki|en|Kawaminami, Miyazaki|WD=Q964194|lt=Kawaminami, Miyazaki|1=Kawaminami, Miyazaki}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th to 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|10|24|N|131|29|48|E}}}}}} {{nihongo|'''Kawaminami Kofun Cluster'''|川南古墳群|}} is a group [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Nishibeppu neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Kawaminami, Miyazaki|en|Kawaminami, Miyazaki|WD=Q964194|lt=Kawaminami, Miyazaki|1=Kawaminami, Miyazaki}} on the island of [[Kyushu]] {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus cluster was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1961.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139794|title=川南古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== A group of ancient tombs located on the edge of the Kokkobaru plateau at an elevation of 50 to 60 meters on the left bank of the Komaru River, and on a plateau one step above the elevation of 80 meters. The cluster consists of 25 {{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. one {{nihongo|''[[Square Kofun|hōfun]]''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} rectangular tomb, and 25 {{nihongo|''[[Round Kofun|enpun]]''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} round tombs within a narrow area of approximately 0.5 square kilometers. There are also several small circular tumuli and undesignated ''kofun'' that are known to have been destroyed, and the area designated as a historical site is limited to the portion containing tumuli that remain visible on the ground. As only a few surveys and [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted, there is a considerable amount of surrounding undesignated area which probably contains more tumuli which remain undiscovered. Since the ratio of {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped tombs|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} in this group is extremely high, it is thought that the tumuli are not the graves of a single chieftain's family, but rather the grave areas of several influential clans over a fairly wide area. The tombs are broadly divided into five groups depending on their location. It is estimated that these tumuli were built between the 4th century and the end of the 6th century, and thus predate the tumuli at the Saitobaru Kofun Cluster. The largest tumulus in the group is Mound No. 39, which has a total length of 112 meters and is surrounded by a moat. It is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Komaru River basin. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From this site the [[Mochida Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 3.5 kilometers to the southeast, the [[Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 9 kilometers to the southwest, and the [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] approximately 8 kilometers to the west-southwest. The site is approximately ten minutes by car from {{ill|Takanabe Station|en|Takanabe Station|WD=Q5357588|lt=Takanabe Station|1=Takanabe Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|WD=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line|1=Nippō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|WD=Q6573277|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)}}

The tumulus cluster was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1961.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139794|title=川南古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== A group of ancient tombs located on the edge of the Kokkobaru plateau at an elevation of 50 to 60 meters on the left bank of the Komaru River, and on a plateau one step above the elevation of 80 meters. The cluster consists of 25 {{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. one {{nihongo|''[[Square Kofun|hōfun]]''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} rectangular tomb, and 25 {{nihongo|''[[Round Kofun|enpun]]''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} round tombs within a narrow area of approximately 0.5 square kilometers. There are also several small circular tumuli and undesignated ''kofun'' that are known to have been destroyed, and the area designated as a historical site is limited to the portion containing tumuli that remain visible on the ground. As only a few surveys and [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted, there is a considerable amount of surrounding undesignated area which probably contains more tumuli which remain undiscovered. Since the ratio of {{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped tombs|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} in this group is extremely high, it is thought that the tumuli are not the graves of a single chieftain's family, but rather the grave areas of several influential clans over a fairly wide area. The tombs are broadly divided into five groups depending on their location. It is estimated that these tumuli were built between the 4th century and the end of the 6th century, and thus predate the tumuli at the Saitobaru Kofun Cluster. The largest tumulus in the group is Mound No. 39, which has a total length of 112 meters and is surrounded by a moat. It is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Komaru River basin. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From this site the [[Mochida Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 3.5 kilometers to the southeast, the [[Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 9 kilometers to the southwest, and the [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] approximately 8 kilometers to the west-southwest. The site is approximately ten minutes by car from {{ill|Takanabe Station|en|Takanabe Station|qid=Q5357588|lt=Takanabe Station|1=Takanabe Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|qid=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|qid=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line|1=Nippō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|qid=Q6573277|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{template-inline|Kawaminami Kofun Cluster}} *[https://www.town.kawaminami.miyazaki.jp/soshiki/12/4356.html Kawaminami town home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mch/details/view/1850 Miyazaki Cultural Heritage] {{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]][[en:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]][[en:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kawaminami Kofun Cluster]] {{Infobox ancient site | name =Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | native_name =川南古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption =Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kawaminami Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|10|24|N|131|29|48|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kawaminami, Miyazaki|en|Kawaminami, Miyazaki|qid=Q964194|lt=Kawaminami, Miyazaki|1=Kawaminami, Miyazaki}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th to 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|10|24|N|131|29|48|E}}}}}} {{nihongo|'''Kawaminami Kofun Cluster'''|川南古墳群|}} is a group [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Nishibeppu neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Kawaminami, Miyazaki|en|Kawaminami, Miyazaki|qid=Q964194|lt=Kawaminami, Miyazaki|1=Kawaminami, Miyazaki}} on the island of [[Kyushu]] {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus cluster was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1961.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139794|title=川南古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>

There are also several small circular tumuli and undesignated ''kofun'' that are known to have been destroyed, and the area designated as a historical site is limited to the portion containing tumuli that remain visible on the ground. As only a few surveys and [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted, there is a considerable amount of surrounding undesignated area which probably contains more tumuli which remain undiscovered. Since the ratio of {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|qid=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped tombs|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}} in this group is extremely high, it is thought that the tumuli are not the graves of a single chieftain's family, but rather the grave areas of several influential clans over a fairly wide area. The tombs are broadly divided into five groups depending on their location. The largest tumulus in the group is Mound No. 39, which has a total length of 112 meters and is surrounded by a moat. From this site the [[Mochida Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 3.5 kilometers to the southeast, the [[Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster]] is approximately 9 kilometers to the southwest, and the [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] approximately 8 kilometers to the west-southwest.
